posts.raw_content:
Hey guys, if you've got friends or family deep in TDS mode, you know they swallow every mainstream narrative hook, line, and sinker. You can't even whisper "Trump" without them flipping out and shutting down.

The trick? Don't go there at all. Sneak in some fun, "off-topic" stuff to get 'em questioning things on their own. Like UFOs. Who doesn't love a good alien conspiracy?

Point 'em to The Why Files on YouTube. It's this awesome channel that dives into UFOs, myths, and weird history in a super entertaining way, but it subtly shows how the "official story" is often full of holes. No politics, just fun facts that make you go "huh?"

The goal is simple: plant that seed of doubt. 

Once they start poking at one narrative, they'll unravel the rest themselves. 

Worked like a charm on my super-lib buddy 3 years back. I just intro'd him to Why Files, he dove headfirst into UFO rabbit holes, started questioning the gov't big time, then realized the media's been straight-up lying about Trump stuff. Boom – by 2024, he voted for the guy.
